details: Systematic co-adsorption and competitive adsorption testing with multi-component gas streams.
Validation in cyclic PSA, TSA, and VSA with real flue gas for techno-economic assessment.
Assess binders, pelletisation pressure, porosity retention, mechanical stability, and adsorption kinetics in shaped forms.
Understand polymer plasticisation and the role of UiO-66 under water, H2S, toluene, and other impurities.
Report physical ageing and MOF-polymer interface stability under pressure and temperature.
Develop thin, defect-free asymmetric or thin-film composite membranes with good particle dispersion and integrity.
Clarify reduction pathways over UiO-66-derived catalytic sites to enable rational design.
Address poor conductivity and limited active-site accessibility.
Reduce reliance on sacrificial donors and organic solvents; prioritise aqueous media or coupled oxidative processes and stronger durability evidence.
Move beyond ideal gas and pure-component tests toward realistic mixtures and cycling.
Report mechanical, thermal, and long-term chemical stability of pellets and membranes.
Link molecular-scale understanding to separations and catalysis design.
Integrate scalable fabrication with process models and system-level assessment.
